delta.description: Skill improves pass rate by 21.5% overall; gains are concentrated in the reorganization task (Eval 0: +43%) where taxonomy enforcement matters. Audit-only task (Eval 2) is near-parity since Claude does that well without guidance.

analyst_notes: The skill's taxonomy enforcement is its biggest differentiator — without it, Claude defaults to sales/collateral/ instead of clients/jacob/deliverables/, which is a meaningful workflow difference.

Eval 2 (audit-only) shows no skill advantage. The assertions don't test for taxonomy knowledge during audits, so the baseline and skill tie. This suggests either: (a) the skill should also teach taxonomy awareness during Phase 1 audit, or (b) these audit-only assertions are non-discriminating and should be replaced with ones that test for taxonomy awareness.

The 'stops before moving' assertion always passes regardless of skill — not discriminating. Consider dropping it from future evals or replacing with something that tests the quality of the approval prompt.
